content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662        322-5660        322-3901        322-6441


April 3, 1984


Supervisor John L. Molinari
City Hall, Room 235
San Francisco, CA 94102

Re: FPPC No. A-84-060

Dear Supervisor Molinari:

This is in reply to your letter dated March 13, 1984,
regarding the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.

You asked whether you must report on your Statement of
Economic Interests a gift of earnings received by your spouse.

Enclosed please find a copy of an opinion issued by the
Commission (Opinion requested by Kenneth Cory, No. 75-094-A,
April 22, 1976) which concludes that gifts received by the spouse
of an official are the separate property of the spouse and do not
have to be disclosed by the official on his or her Statement of
Economic Interests. The opinion states, however, that gifts
ostensibly made to the spouse will be considered a gift to the
official if:

(1) The nature of the gift is such that the official is
likely to enjoy direct benefit or use of the gift to
at least the same extent as the ostensible donee; and

(2) The official in fact enjoys such direct benefit or
use; and

(3) There are no additional circumstances negating an
intent to make an indirect gift to the official.

SUPERVISOR JOHN L. MOLINARI
Room 235, City Hall
San Francisco, California 94102

MAR 13 12 25 PM 84

March 13, 1984


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California  95804

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

     I wish to receive an advisory letter confirming
information received in a telephone conversation with
you regarding the reporting of a gift made to my spouse.

     In 1983, my spouse received a gift of earrings,
valued at $150.00.  It is my understanding that I am
not required to report this gift, regardless of its'
value, because it was given directly to my spouse for
her use exclusively.

     Please advise me of my filing requirements with
regards to this gift at your earliest convenience.


                                        Sincerely,


                                        [signature]
                                        JOHN L. MOLINARI

Supervisor Molinari
April 3, 1984
Page 2


The earrings received by your spouse clearly do not meet
the above criteria. Therefore, they are not a gift to you and
you are not required to report them on your Statement of Economic
Interests.

Please call me if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant

JP:kt
Encl.

sections.question: You asked whether you must report on your Statement of Economic Interests a gift of earrings received by your spouse.
sections.conclusion: The earrings received by your spouse clearly do not meet the above criteria. Therefore, they are not a gift to you and you are not required to report them on your Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a public official report on their Statement of Economic Interests a gift of earrings valued at $150 that was received by their spouse in 1983?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Gifts received by a spouse are not reportable by the official unless the official enjoys direct benefit or use of the gift to at least the same extent as the spouse. Since earrings do not meet this criterion, they need not be reported.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Complete advice letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Document references prior FPPC Opinion 75-094-A. Both the incoming request and FPPC response are included in this document.

QUESTION: You asked whether you must report on your Statement of Economic Interests a gift of earrings received by your spouse.

CONCLUSION: The earrings received by your spouse clearly do not meet the above criteria. Therefore, they are not a gift to you and you are not required to report them on your Statement of Economic Interests.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to Supervisor Molinari's inquiry about whether he must disclose earrings gifted to his spouse on his Statement of Economic Interests. The Commission concludes that spousal gifts are not reportable unless the official directly benefits from them.
